title: chrysanthemum eginense
intro: Chrysanthemum eginense, commonly known as Egin's chrysanthemum or Baisha chrysanthemum, is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family. It is used in traditional Chinese medicine, where it is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and aiding in the treatment of fever and respiratory issues. The therapeutic actions of Chrysanthemum eginense include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immunomodulatory effects, which are attributed to its bioactive constituents, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids. These bioactive compounds have been shown to possess potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities, which contribute to the plant's therapeutic properties.
benefits: Chrysanthemum eginense has many benefits, such as reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health, which can help alleviate symptoms of conditions like arthritis and high blood pressure. Its high antioxidant content can protect against cell damage, potentially reducing the risk of certain cancers and neurodegenerative diseases, such as Alzheimer's and Parkinson's. Additionally, the plant has been shown to have antimicrobial properties, making it effective against a range of infections and promoting wound healing. By incorporating Chrysanthemum eginense into one's life, individuals may experience improved overall well-being, enhanced cognitive function, and a reduced risk of chronic diseases, ultimately leading to a longer and healthier life.
actions: Chrysanthemum eginense has many therapeutic actions, such as anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antiviral properties, which help to reduce swelling, alleviate oxidative stress, and combat viral infections. Its neuroprotective and vasodilatory properties can also improve cognitive function and memory, promote blood flow to the brain, and lower blood pressure. Additionally, chrysanthemum eginense's immunomodulatory and hepatoprotective actions enable it to regulate the immune system and protect the liver from damage, leading to improved overall health and a reduced risk of diseases. By harnessing these therapeutic actions, people can experience improved sleep quality, enhanced physical performance, and increased mental clarity, ultimately leading to a better quality of life.
constituents: Chrysanthemum eginense has many active constituents, such as flavonoids (including quercetin and kaempferol), phenolic acids (like ferulic acid and sinapic acid), terpenoids (including borneol and borneol acetate), and volatile oils (including borneol and camphene). These constituents have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which can improve people's daily lives by reducing the risk of chronic diseases and promoting overall health and well-being. For example, the flavonoids in Chrysanthemum eginense can help to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels, while the phenolic acids can protect against oxidative stress and cell damage. Additionally, the terpenoids and volatile oils in Chrysanthemum eginense have been used to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, by loosening mucus and reducing inflammation.
parts: Chrysanthemum eginense has many medicinal parts, such as its flowers, which are rich in flavonoids like quercetin, and sesquiterpenes like arteannuin B, known for their anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties. The leaves of the plant contain alkaloids and phenolic compounds, including chrysoplenetin and chrysin, which have been shown to possess antioxidant and antimicrobial activities. The roots of Chrysanthemum eginense are reported to contain sesquiterpenes and other terpenoids, such as arteannuin B, which exhibit analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects. Additionally, the stems and leaves of the plant are also used and contain compounds like artemetin and eupatoriopicrin, which may have anti-inflammatory and antitumor properties.
preparations: Chrysanthemum eginense has many herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which have been used to treat various health conditions. Tea preparations are often used to treat fever and reduce inflammation, while tinctures are used to relieve stress and anxiety. Infusions of the plant are also used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, by loosening mucus and reducing congestion. Additionally, chrysanthemum eginense extracts are sometimes used to manufacture capsules and pills, which are used to treat conditions like insomnia and indigestion.
side_effects: Chrysanthemum eginense can have side effects if used improperly, such as causing allergic reactions, gastrointestinal problems, and increased risk of bleeding due to its high content of sesquiterpene lactones. Overconsumption of the plant may lead to symptoms like dizziness, nausea, and vomiting. Long-term use of Chrysanthemum eginense can also cause liver and kidney damage, as well as affect blood sugar levels. In severe cases, improper use of the plant can lead to anaphylaxis, a life-threatening allergic reaction that requires immediate medical attention.
